page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6e.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on a KVM hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the average tier for multi-core operations, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 13967.71 and a PassMark CPU score of 15413.0. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1310.79 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 22.94 GB/sec. Memory performance remains stable in the middle tier, delivering a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 781.7 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 36.65 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and compute-focused resource density make it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without the overhead of high memory allocations. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and database operations, as demonstrated by its static web serving throughput of 11.54 GB/sec and 13.56 million Redis SET operations per second.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6e.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 32.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates under a KVM hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 15413.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 13967.71, contrasted by weaker single-core performance. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, multi-threaded workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is suitable for static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate database operations.
